dispatch: Phase 4 — wire EcologyEntity layer into StreetBlockScene

diff_stat.deletion_audit: The 2 'deletions' are single-line replacements that PARAMETER-EXTEND existing call sites — NO existing logic was removed: (1) `_handlePlantInteract(p.species, p.label)` -> `_handlePlantInteract(p.species, p.label, p.x, p.y)` (added 2 args, original args preserved); (2) `_handlePlantInteract(species, label) {` -> `_handlePlantInteract(species, label, plantX, plantY) {` (added 2 params, original params preserved). All existing branches, dialog flow, forage gate, recovery flow, item grant, and save calls are byte-identical.

registration_notes: registerEntities is bulk-tolerant: configs whose speciesId does not resolve via FLORA_MAP (currently `agave` and `yucca`, which are present in PLANT_ECOLOGY/data layout but not in FLORA's `name` field) are skipped with a console warning. The remaining 6 layout entries (2 mesquite, 2 creosote, prickly_pear, barrel_cactus, jojoba, desert_lavender = 6 entities) register successfully and become part of the per-scene ecology registry. This matches the documented public-API behavior of registerEntities (skip-and-warn rather than tear down the whole scene).

observation_emission_sites.context: Inside _handlePlantInteract, after the existing forage-gate item-grant + dialog code completes successfully (the grantedItem branch). Fires `emitObservation(ecologyEntity, 'interact')`.
Same successful-grant branch. Fires `emitForage(ecologyEntity, 'interact')` for informational record. Existing `state.inventory.push`-style mutation (actually a spread/save) is unchanged.
After the just-show-info dialog branch (no item granted). Fires `emitObservation(ecologyEntity, 'interact')` so observation state advances on inspect-only interactions too.

lookup_strategy: Per-scene Map keyed by `${speciesId}|${x}|${y}`. The `_handlePlantInteract` handler is invoked from `addInteractable.onInteract` — those callbacks now thread `p.x, p.y` into the handler so the matching EcologyEntity can be resolved unambiguously even when multiple instances of the same species exist (e.g. 2 mesquite, 2 creosote).
deep_imports_into_substrate: NONE — only imports from `../systems/ecology/index.js`. No imports from `EcologyEntity.js`, `ecologyState.js`, etc.

untouched_files_audit.other_scenes: NONE modified. Only StreetBlockScene.js was edited.

build.warnings: Only the pre-existing chunk-size warning for vendor-phaser/vendor-rapier; no new warnings introduced.

halt_and_surface_points: The dispatch prompt's example used `type: 'plant'` and source values `'interaction'`/`'forage'`, which do not match the typed surface in systems/ecology/index.js. The agent README's example omits `type` (relying on resolveSpeciesType -> 'flora') and the JSDoc-documented source values are `'proximity'|'interact'|'inspect'|'programmatic'` for emitObservation and `'interact'|'programmatic'` for emitForage. Implementation followed the typed surface and the README example: no `type` field passed, source `'interact'` used everywhere. This is a non-blocking deviation from the prompt verbiage, surfaced here for clarity — runtime behavior is correct.

Layout species `agave` and `yucca` are NOT in FLORA_MAP (not present in src/renderer/game/data/flora.js by `name`); registerEntities will skip them with a console warning per its documented bulk-tolerant behavior. The existing dialog/forage flow for those species still works since `_handlePlantInteract` does not depend on the ecology entity for its primary path. No halt — this matches the documented public-API contract for unknown species in a bulk register call.
runtime_validation_required: User walks up to a known-species plant (e.g. mesquite, creosote, prickly_pear)
Console / event-bus shows `ecology.observation` event firing alongside the dialog
Existing dialog appears with same text as before
If forage gate matches: existing item-grant + `Found X! ...` dialog appears unchanged AND `ecology.forage` event fires
Verify `agave`/`yucca` plants still trigger their existing dialog path (PLANT_INFO entries) even though no ecology entity was registered for them
